text.title: ELK / OpenSearch Centralized Logging Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the elk-stack-logging methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Indices MUST have an Index Lifecycle Management policy (hot/warm/cold/delete) with explicit retention; unbounded retention is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without ILM, indices grow without bound, cluster degrades, and cost grows linearly with time.

rule.statement: Elasticsearch security (xpack.security or OpenSearch security plugin) MUST be enabled; clusters bound to 0.0.0.0 without auth are forbidden.

rationale.source: Elasticsearch security docs, internal incident review

rule.rationale: Unauthenticated public Elasticsearch is one of the most-exploited misconfigurations in the wild.

rule.statement: Applications MUST emit structured logs (JSON with `timestamp`, `level`, `service`, `trace_id`); plain-text logs are forbidden in new pipelines.
rule.rationale: Unstructured logs require regex parsing at ingest; structured logs allow direct indexing and reliable field-level queries.

rule.statement: Logs MUST carry a `trace_id` matching the distributed tracing system; logs without trace_id MUST be flagged for backfill.
rule.rationale: Without trace_id, log search degenerates to grep across service boundaries; with trace_id, an incident query is one click.

rule.statement: Filebeat / Vector agents MUST authenticate to the ingest pipeline via mTLS or API token; unauthenticated ingestion is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Open ingest endpoints are abused by attackers to flood logs (denial-of-wallet) or inject false events.

rule.statement: Kibana MUST ship at least three saved dashboards: per-service error rate, latency percentiles, top-N exceptions; dashboards MUST be version-controlled (NDJSON export).
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc query exploration loses the institutional knowledge of which views matter; saved dashboards are the runbook.

rule.statement: If a managed observability platform already covers logs OR if logs volume < 1GB/day on a single host, this methodology MUST be skipped.
rule.rationale: ELK at small scale is more burden than value; managed platforms cover the same needs cheaper.
